query: What is the maximum capacity threshold for a  generator in MISO to be eligible for the fast-track interconnection process in MISO, according to MISO Tariff Attachment X Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)? Cite the relevant source report and section for your answer
final_answer:
## Maximum Capacity Threshold for Fast-Track Interconnection in MISO

According to the **MISO Tariff Attachment X — Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)**, the maximum capacity threshold for a generator to be eligible for the **Fast Track Process** is **5 MW** (i.e., the generating facility must be no larger than 5 MW).

### Relevant Source and Section

- **Source:** MISO FERC Electric Tariff, **Attachment X — Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)**, ATTACHMENTS 167.0.0 (Effective January 31, 2025). Available at: [https://cdn.misoenergy.org/Attachment%20X%20-%20GIP%20(Effective%20January%2031%202025)330116.pdf](https://cdn.misoenergy.org/Attachment%20X%20-%20GIP%20(Effective%20January%2031%202025)330116.pdf)

- **Relevant Section:** **Section 14** of the GIP governs the interconnection process for **Small Generating Facilities**, which includes the **Fast Track Process**. Section 14 contains four key sub-sections: (1) Applicability, (2) Initial Review, (3) Customer Options Meeting, and (4) Supplemental Review. The Fast Track Process is available for certified Small Generating Facilities that are **no larger than 5 MW**.

### Supporting Evidence

1. **MISO GIP, Section 2.1(b)** states: *"Section 14 of the GIP applies to a request to interconnect a certified Small Generating Facility meeting the certification criteria set forth in Appendix 3."* A "Small Generating Facility" under MISO's GIP is defined as a generating facility with a capacity of **5 MW or less**.

2. **MISO Dashboard (PAC-2025-1)** — "GIA Small Generator Insurance Requirements 5 MW and Under" — explicitly references *"small generators 5 MW and under that are Fast Track eligible"* and *"projects 5 MW and under that are eligible under the Fast Track rules."*
   *(Source: [https://www.misoenergy.org/engage/MISO-Dashboard/gia-small-generator-insurance-requirements-5-mw-and-under/](https://www.misoenergy.org/engage/MISO-Dashboard/gia-small-generator-insurance-requirements-5-mw-and-under/))*

3. **Renewable Energy World** (January 28, 2021) confirms: *"MISO process only discusses 5 MW as the threshold for fast track process applicability"* and *"There is a fast track process for project sizes less than 5 MW with the entire process takes less than 90 days."*
   *(Source: [https://www.renewableenergyworld.com/solar/utility-scale/fast-track-process-is-available-for-renewable-projects-under-5-mw-at-miso-and-spp/](https://www.renewableenergyworld.com/solar/utility-scale/fast-track-process-is-available-for-renewable-projects-under-5-mw-at-miso-and-spp/))*

### Background

The Fast Track Process in MISO's GIP traces its origins to FERC's pro forma **Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)**, established under **FERC Order No. 2006** and subsequently revised by **FERC Order No. 792** (Docket No. RM13-2-000, issued November 22, 2013), which revised the Fast Track threshold upward from the original 2 MW. MISO adopted the **5 MW** threshold in its own GIP under Attachment X.

*(Source: FERC Order No. 792, [https://www.ferc.gov/sites/default/files/2020-06/RM13-2-000_0.pdf](https://www.ferc.gov/sites/default/files/2020-06/RM13-2-000_0.pdf))*

### Summary

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| **Maximum Capacity Threshold** | **5 MW** |
| **Governing Document** | MISO Tariff Attachment X — GIP |
| **Relevant Section** | Section 14 (Fast Track Process for Small Generating Facilities) |
| **Current Effective Version** | ATTACHMENTS 167.0.0 (Effective January 31, 2025) |

### Summary

steps.reasoning:
Based on my research, I can now provide a comprehensive answer. Let me synthesize what I've found:

1. The MISO GIP (Generator Interconnection Procedures) is found in Attachment X of the MISO Tariff.

2. The "Fast Track Process" is addressed in Section 14 of the GIP, which deals with Small Generating Facilities.

3. From the Renewable Energy World article (published January 28, 2021), it clearly states: "Fast Track Process is available for renewable projects under 5 MW at MISO and SPP" and "MISO process only discusses 5 MW as the threshold for fast track process applicability."

4. The MISO Dashboard page on "GIA Small Generator Insurance Requirements 5 MW and Under" (PAC-2025-1) states: "to reduce insurance requirements in LGIA for small generators 5 MW and under that are Fast Track eligible" and "projects 5 mw and under that are eligible under the Fast Track rules."

5. The FERC Order No. 792 (RM13-2-000) revised the Fast Track threshold from 2 MW upward. The order discusses revising "the 2 megawatt (MW) threshold for participation in the Fast Track Process included in section 2 of the pro forma SGIP."

6. The MISO GIP Section 14 covers the process for Small Generating Facilities, including the Fast Track Process. The definition of "Small Generating Facility" in MISO's GIP is a generating facility no larger than 5 MW.

So the maximum capacity threshold for a generator to be eligible for the Fast Track interconnection process in MISO is **5 MW** (or less than or equal to 5 MW).

The relevant source is:
- MISO Tariff Attachment X - Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP), Section 14 (specifically Section 14.2 on Applicability of the Fast Track Process)
- The current effective version is ATTACHMENTS 167.0.0, effective January 31, 2025

Let me also note that the FERC Order No. 792 originally set the pro forma SGIP Fast Track threshold, and MISO adopted the 5 MW threshold in its GIP.

The MISO GIP defines a "Small Generating Facility" as one that is no larger than 5 MW, and Section 14 of the GIP applies to requests to interconnect certified Small Generating Facilities. The Fast Track Process within Section 14 is available for these Small Generating Facilities up to 5 MW.
